correct_answer: The Rosetta Stone became the key to unlocking the secrets of Egyptian hieroglyphic writing.
The meaning of Egyptian hieroglyphs was lost to history before the discovery of the Rosetta Stone.
Scholars and archaeologists had long been intrigued by the symbols and pictograms found on ancient Egyptian monuments.
The breakthrough in deciphering hieroglyphs came in 1822 when Jean-François Champollion successfully deciphered the hieroglyphic text on the Rosetta Stone.
Champollion compared the Greek text with the hieroglyphic and Demotic texts on the Rosetta Stone to decipher the hieroglyphs.
Deciphering the Rosetta Stone opened up a new era of understanding of ancient Egyptian culture, history, and language.

correct_answer: The translation of hieroglyphic texts made possible by the Rosetta Stone revealed insights into the ancient Egyptians' religious beliefs, political structures, and daily life.
The Rosetta Stone inspired new methods and approaches to the study of ancient languages and scripts.
